title: Activist Pineal Capital pushes Teladoc to consider split, other changes to boost value
text: Activist Pineal Capital pushes Teladoc to consider split, other changes to boost value. Summary: Activist investor Pineal Capital Management urged sweeping changes at Teladoc Health, calling for cost cuts and potential breakup to unlock shareholder value. Breakup Strategy: Pineal called for full strategic review including separation of two core businesses - Integrated Care (virtual primary care) and BetterHelp (mental health). Current structure creates conglomerate discount. Breakup could allow each unit independent valuation, unlocking substantial shareholder value. Additional Demands: Authorize $200+ million share repurchase program, arguing company balance sheet is under-leveraged. Push for cost-efficiency measures as BetterHelp shifts toward insurance-backed model. Context: Teladoc stock lost 98% of value from pandemic high in February 2021. Pineal criticized board's past capital allocation, calling 2020 Livongo acquisition overvalued and ill-timed. Criticized lack of share buybacks.
